rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK A STATE CHARTERED BANK VS. RENKA PROP. LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Vacate Order Issuing Writ Of Possession Based On Attorneys Mistake, Inadvertence And Neglect
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday March 8, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANT RENKA PROP LLC Motion To Vacate Order Issuing Writ Of Possession Based On Attorneys Mistake, Inadvertence And Neglect OFF CALENDAR, re-noticed for March 21, 2019. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: TERESITA POBRE VS. WELLS FARGO HOME MORTGAGE, A BUSINESS ENTITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday March 8, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANT U.S. BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS LEGAL TITLE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend. As a preliminary matter, the Court is inclined to (but does not make a determination yet) to follow Manantan v. National City Mortgage 2011 WL 3267706. While this authority is not binding, Plaintiff failed to cite any authority at all to the contrary. However, the Complaint, while suggesting that Plaintiff had actual notice of the sale, is so uncertain that it is not impossible to determine the timing of such actual notice. Opposition makes it even more uncertain. Plaintiff shall have a leave to amend to allege facts (date, time, source of information, date of attempt to tender, etc.) surrounding the allegations in paragraph 20 of the Complaint. If Plaintiff can in good faith, mindful of sham pleading doctrine, allege lack of actual notice, Plaintiff shall also allege facts supporting US Bank's liability as to each cause of action alleged against US Bank, e.g. duty (contractual, statutory, successor liability) to provide new notices and/or serve previously recorded notices, etc. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN PAXTON VS. 330 PRESIDIO AVENUE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday March 8, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT 330 PRESIDIO AVENUE, LLC, JOE TOBONI DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained in part and overruled in part. Joe Toboni's Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts giving rise to individual liability under each cause of action or facts supporting alter ego. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Based on the information available to the Court at the demurrer stage the Court does not determine that collateral estoppel or res judicata applies. While the issues raised in this complaint were raised during administrative proceedings, it cannot be established as a matter of law that they have in fact been adjudicated/considered by the permitting agency or the Board of Appeals in making the determination regarding the permit. Additionally, independent duty and breach of contract are sufficiently alleged. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: FREDERICK RAVANI JR VS. ZACK ZALTSMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday March 8, 2019 line 6. DEFENDANT ZACK ZALTSMAN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden. CC 1942.4 is an affirmative defense and Defendant has the burden of establishing every element of this defense. Defendant fails to produce any evidence re: CC 1942.4(a)(4). Pointing out that Plaintiff has no evidence that Defendant caused the condition could have been enough if the burden was on Plaintiff (e.g. prima facie element of UD), however it is not Plaintiff's burden. Defendant had to present evidence that he did not cause the conditions. Defendant failed to do so. = (501/CFH)
